title: L3: Iteration
description: What loops are, the three loop types in Scratch, and four hands-on builds: repeat loop, forever bounce, key-controlled movement, and a catch game.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now

Answer these five questions from conditionals and variables.
steps.js:
createMCQSet('iter-dna',[{q:'Which block makes code run indefinitely?',opts:[{scratch:'repeat (10)
end'},{scratch:'forever
end'},{scratch:'repeat until <touching [edge v] ?>
end'},{scratch:'if <touching [edge v] ?> then
else
end'}],correct:1,feedback:'Correct - forever repeats without stopping.',wrongFeedback:'The forever loop keeps repeating its contents indefinitely.'},{q:'Which script checks whether the sprite is touching the edge before turning?',opts:[{scratch:'if <touching [edge v] ?> then
  turn cw (180) degrees
end'},{scratch:'forever
  move (5) steps
end'},{scratch:'when green flag clicked
set [score v] to (0)'},{scratch:'change [score v] by (1)'}],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - the condition is inside an if block.',wrongFeedback:'An if block runs its inside blocks only when the condition is true.'},{q:'Which block creates a variable starting value?',opts:[{scratch:'set [score v] to (0)'},{scratch:'change [score v] by (1)'},{scratch:'move (10) steps'},{scratch:'say [score] for (2) seconds'}],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - set gives a variable a specific value.',wrongFeedback:'Use set [score] to (0) at the start to initialise the score.'},{q:'Which block adds one to the current score?',opts:[{scratch:'change [score v] by (1)'},{scratch:'set [score v] to (1)'},{scratch:'set [score v] to (0)'},{scratch:'show variable [score v]'}],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - change by 1 adds to the current value.',wrongFeedback:'change [score] by (1) increases the existing score by one.'},{q:'What does a condition evaluate to?',opts:['True or false','A costume','A sprite name','A sound'],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - conditions are true or false.',wrongFeedback:'Conditions, like touching edge?, are either true or false.'}]);

steps.title: Loop Types
steps.content:
The three loop types

Iteration means repeating instructions using a loop — much cleaner than writing the same blocks over and over.

repeat (5)
  move (10) steps
end

This runs exactly 5 times.

forever
  move (5) steps
  if on edge, bounce
end

This runs until the program stops.

repeat until <touching [edge v]?>
  move (5) steps
end

This runs until the sprite hits the edge.

Choose repeat when you know a fixed count, forever for continuous game loops, and repeat until when you are waiting for something to happen.

steps.title: Build 1: Repeat Loop
steps.content:
Build 1: repeat loop — draw a square

Use a repeat (4) loop to draw a square. Each side needs a move and a turn.

when green flag clicked
pen down
repeat (4)
  move (100) steps
  turn right (90) degrees
end
pen up

The pen blocks are in the Pen extension — click the blue + button at the bottom-left of the block palette to add it.

  1. Add the Pen extension using the blue plus button, then choose Pen
  2. Build the script above
  3. Experiment: change 4 to 3 for a triangle, or 6 for a hexagon

f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame'),w=f.parentElement,b=document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn'),fs=w.classList.toggle('y7-fullscreen');b.textContent=fs?'✕':'⛶';if(fs){window._sy=window.scrollY;document.body.style.overflow='hidden';}else{document.body.style.overflow='';window.scrollTo(0,window._sy||0);}sf();}sf();if(window._sfR)window.removeEventListener('resize',window._sfR);window._sfR=sf;window.addEventListener('resize',sf);if(window._sfK)document.removeEventListener('keydown',window._sfK);window._sfK=function(e){if(e.key==='Escape'){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(f&&f.parentElement.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen'))tfs();}};document.addEventListener('keydown',window._sfK);document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn').onclick=tfs;document.getElementById('y7-check-btn').onclick=function(){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame'),r=document.getElementById('y7-check-result');try{var vm=f.contentWindow.vm;if(!vm){r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Editor still loading.';return;}var T=vm.runtime.targets,hRep=false,hMove=false,hTurn=false;for(var t=0;t<T.length;t++){var B=T[t].blocks._blocks;for(var id in B){var b=B[id];if(b.opcode==='control_repeat')hRep=true;if(b.opcode==='motion_movesteps')hMove=true;if(b.opcode==='motion_turnright'||b.opcode==='motion_turnleft')hTurn=true;}}if(hRep&&hMove&&hTurn){r.style.color='#86efac';r.textContent='✓ Repeat loop with move and turn found!';__markStepComplete();}else{var m=[];if(!hRep)m.push('repeat loop');if(!hMove)m.push('move steps');if(!hTurn)m.push('turn');r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Missing: '+m.join(', ');}}catch(e){r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Error: '+e.message;}};})()

steps.title: Build 2: Arrow Key Controls
steps.content:
Build 2: arrow key controls

This is the standard pattern for player-controlled movement. A forever loop keeps checking the keys every frame.

when green flag clicked
forever
  if <key [right arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (5)
  end
  if <key [left arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (-5)
  end
  if <key [up arrow v] pressed?> then
    change y by (5)
  end
  if <key [down arrow v] pressed?> then
    change y by (-5)
  end
end
  1. Build the script above — four if blocks inside a forever loop
  2. The key pressed? block is in Sensing (light blue)
  3. Run it and move with the arrow keys

steps.title: Build 3: Catch Game
steps.content:
Build 3: catch game

Move the catcher left and right to catch the falling ball. Score a point each catch.

Sprite 1 — Catcher (player)

when green flag clicked
set [score v] to (0)
forever
  if <key [right arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (10)
  end
  if <key [left arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (-10)
  end
end

Sprite 2 — Ball (add a second sprite)

when green flag clicked
go to x: (0) y: (180)
forever
  change y by (-5)
  if <touching [Sprite1 v]?> then
    go to x: (pick random (-200) to (200)) y: (180)
    change [score v] by (1)
  end
  if <(y position) < (-170)> then
    go to x: (pick random (-200) to (200)) y: (180)
  end
end

quizQuestions.q: Which loop runs forever without stopping?
repeat (6) loop runs 'change score by 2'. Score starts at 0. What is score at the end?
Which loop stops when a condition becomes true?
Which script correctly makes a sprite move right when the right arrow is held?
options:
when green flag clicked
move (10) steps

when green flag clicked
forever
  if <key [right arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (5)
  end
end

when [right arrow v] key pressed
move (10) steps

quizQuestions.q: Build a script using a repeat (4) loop that moves the sprite and turns it after each move. Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer:
when green flag clicked
repeat (4)
  move (100) steps
  turn cw (90) degrees
end

quizQuestions.q: Build a script that uses a forever loop to move the sprite and bounce off the edge. Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer:
when green flag clicked
forever
  move (5) steps
  if on edge, bounce
end

quizQuestions.q: Build a sprite that moves right when right arrow is pressed and left when left arrow is pressed (using a forever loop). Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer:
forever
  if <key [right arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (5)
  end
  if <key [left arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (-5)
  end
end
